source: https://homepages.cwi.nl/~aeb/go/games/ (Andries Brouwer's database of go games)

games.title: The Blood-Vomiting Game
games.why: Jowa's three legendary 'ghost moves'. Akaboshi collapsed coughing blood after the four-session game and died weeks later.

games.title: The Ear-Reddening Game
games.why: Shusaku's famous move 127 made Gennan's ears flush red — a spectator doctor predicted the win from it. A cornerstone of classical study.

games.title: The Game of the Century
games.why: Young Go Seigen's shin-fuseki (3-3, star, tengen) against the last hereditary Honinbo; Shusai's camp allegedly found move 160 in group study.

games.title: Honinbo Shusai's Retirement Game
games.why: The end of the old go world, immortalized in Kawabata's novel 'The Master of Go'. Kitani introduced sealed moves.

games.title: The Atomic Bomb Game
games.why: 3rd Honinbo title, game 2, played in the outskirts of Hiroshima; the blast interrupted the game, which was resumed and finished the same day.

games.title: AlphaGo vs Lee Sedol, Game 1
games.why: The first game of the match that changed go forever.

games.title: AlphaGo vs Lee Sedol, Game 2 (Move 37)
games.why: AlphaGo's shoulder hit on move 37 — the most famous AI move in history.

games.title: AlphaGo vs Lee Sedol, Game 4 (God's Touch)
games.why: Lee Sedol's wedge on move 78 — humanity's only win, the 'divine move' of the match.

games.title: AlphaGo vs Lee Sedol, Game 5
games.why: Final game of the historic match, 4-1 to AlphaGo.

games.title: AlphaGo vs Ke Jie, Game 1
games.why: The world #1 against AlphaGo Master; the half-point margin was AlphaGo playing safely, not closely.

games.title: AlphaGo vs Ke Jie, Game 2
games.why: Ke Jie played 'perfectly' for 50 moves by AlphaGo's own estimation.

games.title: AlphaGo vs Ke Jie, Game 3
games.why: AlphaGo's final official game before retirement from competitive play.
